data.flavorText: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon's nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

data.name: Sableye

attacks.name: Go and Collect
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

data.flavorText: It feeds on gemstone crystals. In darkness, its eyes sparkle with the glitter of jewels.

data.name: Woobat

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

data.flavorText: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

data.name: Golurk V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rewind Beam
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

data.name: Florges

abilities.name: Rapid Strike Connection
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move an Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your Rapid Strike Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

data.flavorText: Its life can span several hundred years. It's said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens.

data.name: Flabébé

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

data.flavorText: Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it's collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties.

data.name: Floette

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

data.flavorText: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.

data.name: Sylveon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dream Gift
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

data.name: Sylveon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Precious Touch
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Harmony
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

data.name: Pumpkaboo

abilities.name: Pumpkin Pit
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Stadium in play.

data.flavorText: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

data.name: Gourgeist

attacks.name: Pandemonium
attacks.text: Reveal the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each Psychic Pokémon you find there. Then, shuffle those Pokémon back into your deck and discard the other cards.

data.flavorText: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.

data.name: Cutiefly
data.flavorText: An opponent's aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent's next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.

data.name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Clean Hit
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Bullet Straight Punch
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

data.flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

data.name: Ribombee

attacks.name: Tricky Steps
attacks.text: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.

data.name: Marshadow

attacks.name: Rapid Hunt
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Rapid Strike cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Shadow Flicker
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out during your next turn, take 1 more Prize card.

data.flavorText: It sinks into the shadows of people and Pokémon, where it can understand their feelings and copy their capabilities.

data.name: Medicham V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Yoga Loop
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take another turn after this one. (Skip Pokémon Checkup.) If 1 of your Pokémon used Yoga Loop during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

attacks.name: Smash Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

data.name: Hippopotas
data.flavorText: This Pokémon is active during the day and passes the cold desert nights burrowed snugly into the sand.

data.name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Sand Press
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

data.flavorText: When roused to violence by its rage, it spews out the quantities of sand it has swallowed and whips up a sandstorm.

data.name: Galarian Zapdos

abilities.name: Strong Legs Charge
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Fighting Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zapper Kick
attacks.text: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: One kick from its powerful legs will pulverize a dump truck. Supposedly, this Pokémon runs through the mountains at over 180 mph.

data.name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Pressure Shot
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

data.flavorText: Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.

data.name: Roggenrola
data.flavorText: When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.

data.name: Boldore

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

data.flavorText: It relies on sound in order to monitor what's in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it's facing.

data.name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Shaky Wave
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

data.flavorText: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.

data.name: Palpitoad
data.flavorText: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

data.name: Lycanroc V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crashing Fangs
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

data.name: Umbreon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Moonlight Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

data.name: Lycanroc VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hunting Claw
attacks.text: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has 60 HP or less remaining.

attacks.name: Max Edge
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

data.name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: They live in holes bored in large trees. The sound of Nuzleaf's grass flute fills listeners with dread.

data.name: Umbreon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Signal
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

data.name: Galarian Moltres

abilities.name: Malevolent Charge
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Darkness Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fiery Wrath
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

data.flavorText: The sinister aura that blazes like molten fire around this Pokémon is what inspired the name Moltres.

data.name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

data.flavorText: While mostly known for having the temperament of an aggressive ruffian, this Pokémon takes very good care of its family, friends, and territory.

data.name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Hard Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

data.flavorText: It protects itself with its durable skin. It's thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.

data.name: Shiftry

attacks.name: Shiftadieu
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any damage counters on it, put it and all attached cards into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Nipping Cyclone
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

data.flavorText: It lives quietly in the deep forest. It is said to create chilly winter winds with the fans it holds.

data.name: Zoroark

abilities.name: Phantom Transformation
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may choose a Stage 1 Pokémon, except any Zoroark, from your discard pile. If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards, and put the chosen Pokémon in its place.

data.flavorText: If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.

data.name: Zorua
data.flavorText: If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.

data.name: Garbodor V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trash Stench
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

data.name: Garbodor VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Rubbish Collecting
abilities.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to it. If it loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tools from it until only 1 remains.

attacks.name: G-Max Malodor
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

data.name: Altaria

abilities.name: Tempting Tune
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.

data.flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

data.name: Nickit
data.flavorText: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

data.name: Thievul

abilities.name: Fumbling Hands
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have each player shuffle their hand and put it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player draws 4 cards.

data.flavorText: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

data.name: Shelgon

attacks.name: Hard Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Shelgon lives deep within caves. It stays shut up in its hard shell, dreaming of the day it will be able to fly.

data.name: Bagon
data.flavorText: Some theories suggest that its behavior of forcefully bashing its head into things stimulates cells that affect its evolution.

data.name: Salamence

abilities.name: Intimidating Roar
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fierce Dragon
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

data.flavorText: Thanks to its fervent wishes, the cells in its body finally mutated, and at last it has its heart's desire—wings.

data.name: Rayquaza VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Azure Pulse
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Max Burst
attacks.text: You may discard any amount of basic Fire Energy or any amount of basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

data.name: Dialga

attacks.name: Chrono Wind
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

data.flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

data.name: Deino

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.flavorText: Because it can't see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings.

data.name: Rayquaza V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Burst
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 basic Fire Energy or up to 2 basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

data.name: Kyurem

attacks.name: Extreme Freeze
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Water Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

data.flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

data.name: Hydreigon

attacks.name: Dragon Counter
attacks.text: This attack does 100 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took during their last turn.

data.flavorText: The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won't slow until their target goes down.

data.name: Noivern V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Boomburst
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Synchro Loud
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 120 more damage.

data.name: Zweilous
data.flavorText: Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others.

data.name: Drampa

attacks.name: Berserk
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

data.flavorText: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it's angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

data.name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Judgment Surge
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

data.flavorText: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.

data.name: Flapple

attacks.name: Acidic Mucus
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has an Ability.

attacks.name: Fighting Tackle
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.

data.flavorText: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

data.name: Appletun

attacks.name: Thick Mucus
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Special Energy card attached to your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Tackle
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.

data.flavorText: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

data.name: Duraludon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Breaking Swipe
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

data.name: Regidrago

attacks.name: Dragon Energy
attacks.text: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Its body is composed of crystallized dragon energy. Regidrago is said to have the powers of every dragon Pokémon.

data.name: Duraludon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Skyscraper
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have Special Energy attached.

attacks.name: G-Max Pulverization
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

data.name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Live Painting
attacks.text: Reveal any number of basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy you revealed in this way.

data.flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

data.name: Teddiursa
data.flavorText: Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

data.name: Slakoth

attacks.name: Smack 'n' Slack
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

data.flavorText: If it eats just three leaves in a day, it is satisfied. Other than that, it sleeps for 20 hours a day.

data.name: Vigoroth

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

data.flavorText: Its stress level rises if it cannot keep moving constantly. Too much stress makes it feel sick.

data.name: Eevee

attacks.name: Vee-Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

data.name: Ursaring
data.flavorText: Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.

data.name: Slaking

abilities.name: Act Freely
abilities.text: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

data.flavorText: It is the world's most slothful Pokémon. However, it can exert horrifying power by releasing pent-up energy all at once.

data.name: Swablu

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

data.flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

data.name: Fletchling

attacks.name: Tailwind Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card. If you go second and it's your first turn, draw 3 more cards.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

data.flavorText: Its body is always warm. Trainers who live in cold areas apparently sleep with it in their bed.

data.name: Herdier

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: It has been living with people for so long that portrayals of it can be found on the walls of caves from long, long ago.

data.name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.flavorText: This Pokémon is popular with beginners because it's intelligent, obedient to its Trainer's commands, and easy to raise.

data.name: Rufflet

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

data.flavorText: A combative Pokémon, it's ready to pick a fight with anyone. It has talons that can crush hard berries.

data.name: Braviary

attacks.name: Valiant Talons
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: Because this Pokémon is hotheaded and belligerent, it's Corviknight that's taken the role of transportation in Galar.

data.name: Fletchinder

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

data.flavorText: Its speed right after takeoff already puts it in the top speed class of all bird Pokémon.

data.name: Stoutland

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.

data.flavorText: Its fur is long and thick. A long time ago in cold regions, every household kept a Stoutland.

data.name: Aroma Lady
rules: Draw 2 cards. If you do, your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Talonflame

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Nitro Dive
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

data.flavorText: Bird Pokémon make up most of its diet. It approaches at high speeds and smacks them down to the ground with its powerful kick.

data.name: Crystal Cave
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may heal 30 damage from each of their Metal Pokémon and Dragon Pokémon.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Copycat

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

rules: You can play this card only if you took it as a face-down Prize card, before you put it into your hand. Search your deck for a Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Gordie

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

rules: Heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, flip a coin. If heads, put this Lucky Ice Pop into your hand instead of the discard pile.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Moon & Sun Badge

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

data.name: Rubber Gloves

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Ribbon Badge

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

data.name: Shopping Center

data.name: Single Strike Scroll of the Fanged Dragon
rules: The Single Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

attacks.name: Superstrong Slash
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), your opponent discards a card from their hand.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

data.name: Zinnia's Resolve

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Switching Cups
rules: Switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Stormy Mountains
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic Lightning Pokémon or Basic Dragon Pokémon and put it onto their Bench. Then, that player shuffles their deck.

data.name: Leafeon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Greening Cells

data.flavorText: It spits out cold air of nearly -60 degrees Fahrenheit to freeze its quarry. It brings frozen prey back to its lair and neatly lines them up.

data.flavorText: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes' weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

data.name: Alakazam

abilities.name: Damage Swap
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to another as long as you don't Knock Out that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Alakazam is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

data.flavorText: Its brain can outperform a supercomputer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5000.

data.name: Blastoise

abilities.name: Rain Dance
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 1 Water Energy card to 1 of your Water Pokémon. (This doesn't use up your 1 Energy card attachment for the turn.) This power can't be used if Blastoise is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

data.flavorText: A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They are used for high-speed tackles.

data.name: Chansey

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Chansey during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Chansey does 80 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: A rare and elusive Pokémon that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to catch it.

data.name: Charizard

abilities.name: Energy Burn
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all Energy attached to Charizard into Fire Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can't be used if Charizard is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard in order to use this attack.

data.flavorText: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

data.name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefairy's type is still Colorless.)

data.flavorText: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

data.name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

data.name: Hitmonchan
data.flavorText: While seeming to do nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.

data.name: Machamp

abilities.name: Strikes Back
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent's attack damages Machamp (even if Machamp is Knocked Out), this power does 10 damage to the attacking Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) This power can't be used if Machamp is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed when your opponent attacks.

data.flavorText: Using its amazing muscles, it throws powerful punches that can knock its victim clear over the horizon.

data.name: Magneton

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 80 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. It frequently appears when sunspots flare up.

data.name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Barrier
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Mewtwo in order to prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mewtwo during your opponent's next turn.

data.flavorText: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

data.name: Nidoking

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

data.flavorText: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

data.name: Ninetales

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Ninetales in order to use this attack.

data.flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

data.name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwrath but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd doesn't count.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

data.flavorText: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

data.name: Raichu

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Raichu.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Raichu does 30 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

data.flavorText: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

data.name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos in order to use this attack.

data.flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

data.name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

data.flavorText: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

data.name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

data.flavorText: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

data.name: Dugtrio

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

data.flavorText: A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.

data.name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunderpunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage plus Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: Normally found near power plants, it can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

data.name: Electrode

abilities.name: Buzzap
abilities.text: At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may Knock Out Electrode and attach it to 1 of your other Pokémon. If you do, choose a type of Energy. Electrode is now an Energy card (instead of a Pokémon) that provides 2 energy of that type. You can't use this power if Electrode is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electric Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrode does 10 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

data.name: Pidgeotto

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mirror Move
attacks.text: If Pidgeotto was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Pidgeotto to the Defending Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Very protective of its sprawling territory, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

data.name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Arcanine in order to use this attack.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Arcanine does 30 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

data.name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon in order to use this attack.

data.flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

data.name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

data.name: Dratini
data.flavorText: Long considered a mythical Pokémon until recently, when a small colony was found living underwater.

data.name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Leek Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. Either way, you can't use this attack again as long as Farfetch'd stays in play (even putting Farfetch'd on the Bench won't let you use it again).

data.flavorText: The sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. This sprig is used much like a metal sword.

data.name: Growlithe
data.flavorText: Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

data.name: Haunter

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dream Eater
attacks.text: You can't use this attack unless the Defending Pokémon is Asleep.

data.flavorText: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.

data.name: Ivysaur

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

data.flavorText: When the bulb on its back grows large, the Pokémon seems to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

data.name: Jynx

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Merely by meditating, the Pokémon launches a powerful psychic energy attack.

data.name: Kadabra

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Kadabra in order to use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Kadabra.

data.flavorText: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches even to those just nearby.

data.name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Kakuna during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

data.flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

data.name: Machoke

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 50 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machoke.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Machoke does 20 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

data.name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.

data.flavorText: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

data.name: Magmar

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Magmar in order to use this attack.

data.flavorText: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

data.name: Nidorino

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

data.flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

data.name: Poliwhirl

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

data.flavorText: Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.

data.name: Porygon

attacks.name: Conversion 1
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has a Weakness, you may change it to a type of your choice other than Colorless.

attacks.name: Conversion 2
attacks.text: Change Porygon's Resistance to a type of your choice other than Colorless.

data.flavorText: A Pokémon that consists entirely of programming code. Capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

data.name: Raticate

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.text: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).

data.flavorText: It uses its whiskers to maintain its balance. It seems to slow down if they are cut off.

data.name: Seel
data.flavorText: The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.

data.name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Wartortle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

data.flavorText: Often hides in water to stalk unweary prey. When swimming quickly, it moves its ears to maintain balance.

data.name: Abra

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

data.name: Bulbasaur

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.

data.flavorText: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

data.name: Caterpie

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

data.name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmander in order to use this attack.

data.flavorText: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

data.name: Diglett
data.flavorText: Lives about three feet underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears above ground.

data.name: Doduo

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

data.flavorText: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

data.name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

data.flavorText: Puts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.

data.name: Gastly

attacks.name: Sleeping Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Destiny Bond
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Gastly in order to use this attack. If a Pokémon Knocks Out Gastly during your opponent's next turn, Knock Out that Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Almost invisible, this gaseous Pokémon cloaks the target and puts it to sleep without notice.

data.name: Koffing

attacks.name: Foul Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, it is now Confused.

data.flavorText: Because it stores several kinds of toxic gases in its body, it is prone to exploding without warning.

data.name: Machop
data.flavorText: Loves to build its muscles. It trains in all styles of martial arts to become even stronger.

data.name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magnemite does 40 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses attacks like Thunder Wave.

data.name: Metapod

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Metapod during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: It is vulnerable to attack because its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

data.name: Nidoran ♂

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

data.flavorText: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger, more powerful of its horns secretes venom.

data.name: Onix

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Onix (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

data.flavorText: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, though colored black.

data.name: Pidgey

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

data.flavorText: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

data.name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

data.name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwag but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd don't count.

data.flavorText: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming over trying to stand.

data.name: Ponyta
data.flavorText: Its hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything flat in moments.

data.name: Rattata
data.flavorText: Bites anything when it attacks. Small and very quick, it is a common sight in many places.

data.name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

data.flavorText: Burrows deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

data.name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Squirtle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

data.flavorText: After birth, its back swells and hardens into a shell. It powerfully sprays foam from its mouth.

data.name: Starmie

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard 1 Water Energy card attached to Starmie in order to use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Starmie.

attacks.name: Star Freeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value this core as a gem.

data.name: Staryu
data.flavorText: An enigmatic Pokémon that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.

data.name: Tangela

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

data.flavorText: Its whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. These vines shake as it walks.

data.name: Voltorb
data.flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Monster Ball, it has zapped many people.

data.name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

data.flavorText: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.

data.name: Weedle

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

data.flavorText: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp, venomous stinger on its head.

data.name: Clefairy Doll
rules: Play Clefairy Doll as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Clefairy Doll counts a a Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Clefairy Doll has no attacks, can't retreat, and can't be Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. If Clefairy Doll is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Clefairy Doll.

data.name: Computer Search
rules: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to search your deck for any card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

data.name: Devolution Spray
rules: Choose 1 of your own Pokémon in play and a Stage of Evolution. Discard all Evolution cards of that Stage or higher attached to that Pokémon. That Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or anything else that might be the result of an attack (just as if you had evolved it).

data.name: Impostor Professor Oak
rules: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 7 cards.

data.name: Item Finder
rules: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

data.name: Lass
rules: You and your opponent show each other your hands, then shuffle all the Trainer cards from your hands into your decks.

data.name: Pokémon Breeder
rules: Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can only play this card when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.

data.name: Pokémon Trader
rules: Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.

data.name: Scoop Up
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)

data.name: Super Energy Removal
rules: Discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon in order to choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and up to 2 Energy cards attached to it. Discard those Energy cards.

data.name: Defender
rules: Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard Defender. Damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

data.name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Trade 1 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile.

data.name: Full Heal
rules: Your Active Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

data.name: Maintenance
rules: Shuffle 2 of the other cards from your hand into your deck in order to draw a card.

data.name: PlusPower
rules: Attach PlusPower to your Active Pokémon. At the end of your turn, discard PlusPower. If this Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

data.name: Pokémon Center
rules: Remove all damage counters from all of your own Pokémon with damage counters on them, then discard all Energy cards attached to those Pokémon.

data.name: Pokémon Flute
rules: Choose 1 Basic Pokémon card from your opponent's discard pile and put it onto his or her Bench. (You can't play Pokémon Flute if your opponent's Bench is full.)

data.name: Pokédex
rules: Look at up to 5 cards from the top of your deck and rearrange them as you like.

data.name: Professor Oak
rules: Discard your hand, then draw 7 cards.

data.name: Revive
rules: Put 1 Basic Pokémon card from your discard pile onto your Bench. Put damage counters on that Pokémon equal to half its HP (rounded down to the nearest 10). (You can't play Revive if your Bench is full.)

data.name: Super Potion
rules: Discard 1 Energy card attached to your own Pokémon in order to remove up to 4 damage counters from that Pokémon.

data.name: Bill
rules: Draw 2 cards.

data.name: Energy Removal
rules: Choose 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and discard it.

data.name: Gust of Wind
rules: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.

data.name: Potion
rules: Remove up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

data.name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your own Benched Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

data.name: Double Colorless Energy
rules:
Provides ColorlessColorless energy.
Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.
